posts.name: Robert M yers
posts.cooked:
What will I be sorry I didn’t buy from this list 10-25 years down the road, if anything. Many more to come I just cherry picked some that looked like decent deals. Let me know what you burgheads think.

2017 Bachelet Cote Nuits $55
2017 Denis Bachelet Gevrey-Chambertin Les Evocelles $110
2017 G-C VV $90
2017 Bourgone $45

2017 Sylvain Cathiard Nuits St Georges Aux Thorey $200
2017 Domaine Sylvain Cathiard Vosne Romanee $135

2015 Domaine Jerome Chezeaux Nuits St. Georges Rue de Chaux $50
2014/2016 Domaine Jerome Chezeaux Clos Vougeot $90/$105
2015 Domaine Jerome Chezeaux Nuits St. Georges Les Charbonnieres $42
2015/2016 Domaine Jerome Chezeaux Nuits St. Georges Rue de Chaux $50/$55

2016 Domaine Pierre-Yves Colin-Morey Nuits-St.-Georges $51

2015/2017 Robert Groffier Pere & Fils Chambolle Musigny 1er Cru Hauts Doix $126/$140
2015/2017 Domaine Robert Groffier Pere & Fils Chambolle Musigny Les Sentiers $130/147

2016/17 Domaine AF Gros Chambolle Musigny $62/$60
2013/15/16 Anne Gros Echezeaux Loachausses Grand Cru $155
14/15/16 Anne Gros Clos Vougeot Le Grand Maupertui $168

2016 Hudelot-Noellat Nuits St Georges Les Murgers $81
2016 Hudelot-Noellat Vosne Romanee Les Suchots 1er Cru $157
2012/13/14/2016 Hudelot-Noellat Clos Vougeot $150-160

2014/16 Lucien Le Moine Chambolle Musigny Haut Doix $116/$124
2105 Maison Lucien Le Moine Gevrey Chambertin Les Combottes $105
2015 Maison Lucien Le Moine Volnay Clos des Chenes $67

2016 Thibault Liger Belair Corton Les Renardes $140

2016 Marchand Tawse Vosne Romanee $53
2015/16 Marchand Tawse Echezeaux $158/$175
2015 Marchand Tawse Charmes Chambertin $147

2015 Meo-Camuzet Clos Vougeot $239
2014/17 Domaine Meo-Camuzet Vosne-Romanee Les Chaumes $134/$153

2016 Mongeard Mugneret Grands Echezeaux $200

2015 Domaine Ponsot Morey-Saint-Denis Cuvee des Alouettes $105

2015/16 Domaine Jean-Claude Ramonet Chassagne Montrachet Morgeot Rouge $70
2015 Domaine Jean-Claude Ramonet Chassagne Montrachet Clos de la Boudriotte Rouge $77

2017 Domaine Gerard Raphet Clos Vougeot $117
2017 Domaine Gerard Raphet Clos de la Roche $134

12/13/15/17 Domaine Bruno Clavelier Corton Le Rognet Vielles Vignes $155-160
2017 Domaine Bruno Clavelier Vosne Romanee La Combe Brulee $90
2013/14/17 Domaine Bruno Clavelier Vosne Romanee Les Beaux Monts $123/$148

posts.name: Mar.kus Meer
posts.cooked: From a value perspective, I’d go Anne Gros Echezeaux and wouldn’t bother with any of the others.

posts.name: Mark Thompson
posts.cooked: I can’t speak to much of that list but I would jump at the Meo Clos Vougeot.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Bachelet’s 2017s turned out brilliantly and will offer broad drinking windows, warmly recommended. The Cathiard 2017s are similarly exquisite and in the context of contemporary pricing for that domaine’s wines, why not. And the Hudelot-Noëllat 2016s are great, adapted to extended cellaring if you have the patience for it.

posts.name: Howard Cooper
posts.cooked:
My favorite producers on the list are Bachelet and Hudelot-Noellat.

But the sleepers in the group IMHO are the Ramonet reds. These wines seem to be going up in price (unless you can buy out of Burgundy) and getting harder to find over time.

posts.name: Mich@el Ch@ng
posts.cooked: H&Ns (all of them), Ramonet reds, clavelier Combe brulee

posts.name: D Borczon
posts.cooked: To be a contrarian, where are all these producers going to sell their wine if the bottom drops out of the global economy in 6 months? Not to mention retailers. I for one, am waiting. The old saw of “you can’t catch a falling knife” in the stock market does not apply when this is a guillotine.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
To be a contrarian, where are all these producers going to sell their wine if the bottom drops out of the global economy in 6 months? Not to mention retailers. I for one, am waiting. The old saw of “you can’t catch a falling knife” in the stock market does not apply when this is a guillotine.

HK / China / Japan market still very robust for producers such as these. It’s things like Mâcon and Beaujolais that will tank.

posts.name: Brian Gilp
posts.cooked:
To be a contrarian, where are all these producers going to sell their wine if the bottom drops out of the global economy in 6 months? Not to mention retailers. I for one, am waiting. The old saw of “you can’t catch a falling knife” in the stock market does not apply when this is a guillotine.

HK / China / Japan market still very robust for producers such as these. It’s things like Mâcon and Beaujolais that will tank.

While this most likely will be what happens, we are still in the early innings of both the pandemic and the economic crisis that may well result in a worldwide depression. And if that does happen, I believe all bets are off as to what holds prices and what collapses.

posts.name: Robert M yers
posts.cooked:
To be a contrarian, where are all these producers going to sell their wine if the bottom drops out of the global economy in 6 months? Not to mention retailers. I for one, am waiting. The old saw of “you can’t catch a falling knife” in the stock market does not apply when this is a guillotine.

Valid point in all things today, but I have a rather large credit that I would like to use. So help me me spend some “money”.

posts.name: alan weinberg
posts.cooked:
H&Ns (all of them), Ramonet reds, clavelier Combe brulee

+1

posts.name: Alexander Gillette
posts.cooked:
If you are trying to just take advantage of deals, I’d wait 9 months. It’s gonna be a bloodbath out there. Think about the effect that every fine dining restaurant in the world shutting down will have on the price of wine.
A

posts.name: Robert M yers
posts.cooked:
If you are trying to just take advantage of deals, I’d wait 9 months. It’s gonna be a bloodbath out there. Think about the effect that every fine dining restaurant in the world shutting down will have on the price of wine.
A

I have a bunch of $ on credit. I don’t want to wait with uncertainties todays market, bird in the hand and all. I was offered a nice discount and these are some of the better choices.
If anybody is really bored locked up at home I can send the full list and help me sort through. Big listing of white burgs too.

13 chave @ $200
16 clape @ $125
16 Jamet @$150 ect
Are some choices too.
